chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. C. P. No. D – 4554 of 2023 ____________________________________________________________ Dated Order with signature of Judge. ____________________________________________________________ Fresh case 1. For order on Misc. No.21125/2023 2. For order on office objection No.19 3. For order on Misc. No.21126/2023 4. For order on Misc. No.21127/2023 5. For order on Misc. No.21128/2023 6. For hearing of main case. 26.09.2023. The Petitioners are present in person. --------------- YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J.- The Petitioner No.1 is a Private Limited Company carrying on business as a travel agent, whereas the Petitioners Nos.2 to 4 purport to be its Chief Executive, Director and Manager respectively. The case advanced by them is that the Petitioner No.1 entered into a Service Provider Agreement with the Government of Pakistan on 08.06.2023 through its Ministry of Religious Affairs & Interfaith Harmony (Respondent No.1) in respect of the Hajj operation for the year 2023, pursuant to which it was allocated a quota for 50 pilgrims with Hajj package Agreements/contracts then separately being entered into by the Petitioner No.1 with various individuals up to that threshold. Their grievance is that the Petitioner No.1 is being called upon by the Respondent No.1 and its functionaries to responds to the complaints of persons beyond the 50 individuals with whom Agreements/Contracts were entered into. It is contended that the Respondents are thus acting beyond their jurisdiction, with it being prayed that the so-called notices of meetings and phone calls issued/made in that regard be declared unconstitutional and unlawful.

Their grievance is that the Petitioner No.1 is being called upon by the Respondent No.1 and its functionaries to responds to the complaints of persons beyond the 50 individuals with whom Agreements/Contracts were entered into. It is contended that the Respondents are thus acting beyond their jurisdiction, with it being prayed that the so-called notices of meetings and phone calls issued/made in that regard be declared unconstitutional and unlawful. We have examined the notices which has been filed along with the Petition, and observed that the same merely call upon the Petitioners to attend a meeting of the Complaint Disposal Committee and submit their written reply. As such no definite action has been taken in the matter so as to infringe any fundamental right and warrant interference by this Court in exercise of its constitutional jurisdiction. Thus, the matter does not present a fit case for issuance of writ. As such, while granting the application for urgency, we hereby dismiss the Petition in limine alongwith the other pending miscellaneous applications. Judge Judge TariqAli/PA
